content.full_text:
April 19, 1996


Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk
555 Capitol Mall
Suite 530
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Letter No. 95-139

Dear Mr. Bell, Jr.:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on April 19, 1995, by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Daniel Maullem, an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature] Steven G. Churchwell
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

SGC:ak




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: No substantive legal question is presented in this document; this is an acknowledgment letter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a procedural acknowledgment/receipt letter, not a substantive advice response. No question or conclusion sections exist in the document. The actual advice request letter is not included. Document dated April 19, 1996 but references receipt date of April 19, 1995.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC acknowledgment letter confirming receipt of an advice request (Letter No. 95-139) from Charles H. Bell, Jr. on April 19, 1995. The letter explains the FPPC's timeline for responding to advice requests and notes that the request and response will be public records.
